reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_218]]
Speaker 1: It truly is a beautiful moment, when students can come together with their families to celebrate this hard-earned and significant achievement.

[[BLOCK:srt_219]]
Speaker 1: And the vibrant atmosphere of attending the commencement ceremony in San Francisco is something few other occasions can match.

[[BLOCK:srt_220]]
Speaker 1: We usually hold the ceremony in a baseball stadium, filled with graduates and their families.

[[BLOCK:srt_221]]
Speaker 1: To experience that moment in person and genuinely feel that you are part of this community is something incredibly special and rare.

[[BLOCK:srt_222]]
Speaker 1: Many of our students are working professionals, spread across the United States and around the world.

[[BLOCK:srt_223]]
Speaker 1: When everyone gathers to celebrate their achievements and the strength of our global community, the moment carries extraordinary meaning.

[[BLOCK:srt_224]]
Speaker 1: The commencement ceremony at Golden Gate University truly stands apart.

[[BLOCK:srt_225]]
Speaker 1: I have worked at several universities, but when you stand there and look out at the incredible global diversity of our student body, the sense of awe and emotion is almost impossible to put into words.

[[BLOCK:srt_226]]
Speaker 1: We sincerely hope our students can attend in person.

[[BLOCK:srt_227]]
Speaker 3: Do you personally present their diplomas?

[[BLOCK:srt_228]]
Speaker 1: When students attend in person, they walk across the stage, shake hands with the provost and the president, and most importantly, are recognized and celebrated before the entire audience for this major milestone in their lives.

[[BLOCK:srt_229]]
Speaker 1: Of course, we value the opportunity to shake their hands and congratulate them ourselves, but what matters most is being there to share in their moment of celebration.
